name: Guba
description: Guba, located in the foothills of the Greater Caucasus Mountains, is a gateway to Azerbaijan's most stunning natural landscapes and ancient mountain villages. The region is famous for the breathtaking Khinalig village, one of the highest permanently inhabited settlements in Europe, and the magnificent Guba Waterfall. Visitors can explore traditional carpet weaving workshops, taste authentic mountain cuisine, and experience the unique culture of the Lezgin and other ethnic communities.

tips: Visit during May‑October for the best weather.
Carry local currency (AZN) as some places may not accept cards.
Respect local customs and dress modestly when visiting religious sites.
touristMistakes: Not allocating enough time to explore the surrounding region.
Overlooking local cuisine and sticking to familiar foods.
Assuming all attractions are within walking distance – plan transportation.
hiddenGems: Scenic viewpoints outside the main tourist areas.
Traditional workshops where you can see crafts being made.
content.howToGetThere: From Baku: 170 km via Car. The journey takes approximately 3 hours by road.
